title: The Sunday Money Date for Couples
subtitle: A weekly ritual for the partnership where money has been a hard topic
excerpt: A weekly half-hour ritual for couples who find money a hard topic. Same time, same place, a short shared agenda, and a hard close at thirty minutes. Used to turn the big charged money talk into a small steady habit.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The weekly money meeting shape is a staple of writing on couples and money, including the work of Bari Tessler.

practice.whenToUse: Use weekly, ideally on Sunday morning or a similar quiet slot. Especially useful when money has been a charged topic that only comes up under pressure.

content.text: A weekly half-hour ritual for couples who find money a hard topic. Same time, same place, a short shared agenda, a hard stop at thirty minutes. The small weekly habit replaces the big charged talk that only happens under pressure.
New to activity as a practice? Read

Most couples talk about money only when something has gone wrong. The talk lands at the wrong time. One person is tired. The other has been carrying the worry alone for a week. The talk goes sideways fast.
A weekly meeting takes the heat out. The money talk has its own slot. The rest of the week is free of it.
Money decisions are made small and made together. The big talks do not need to happen because the small ones already have. The worry does not build up because it has a weekly outlet.
The half hour is also a regular date with one part of the shared life. It can become a habit that holds, even when the rest of the week is busy.
Pick the time. Same slot each week. Sunday morning over coffee works for many couples. Friday evening before dinner works for others.

Set the agenda the same way each time. Five minutes of cash flow this week. Five minutes of bills due next week. Five minutes of a planned spend or a shared goal. Five minutes of a question one of you has been carrying. The rest is buffer.
Set a timer for thirty minutes. When the timer goes, the talk closes.
End with one thing you have agreed on, even if it is small. "We are going to leave the savings as is for two more weeks." "We are spending less on takeaway next week."
If the half hour goes sideways, the agreement is to come back to it next week, not to keep going. The hard stop is the practice.
Original to homemade.education. The weekly money meeting is a staple of writing on couples and money. The work of Bari Tessler is a useful starting point.
